content.problemStatement:
You are given an array arrarrarr where arr[i]arr[i]arr[i] represents the number of pages in the iii-th book, and mmm students. You need to allocate all books to the students such that:


Each student gets at least one book
Each book should be allocated to exactly one student
Books must be allocated in a contiguous manner (consecutive books)
Find the minimum possible value of the maximum number of pages allocated to any student. If it's impossible to allocate books according to the given conditions, return -1.


Input Format

First line contains TTT - number of test cases
For each test case:

First line contains two integers NNN and MMM - number of books and number of students
Second line contains NNN space-separated integers arr[i]arr[i]arr[i] - number of pages in each book




Output Format

For each test case, print a single integer representing the minimum possible maximum pages allocated to any student, or -1 if allocation is impossible

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1:
Possible Allocations:

{12, 34} | {67, 90} ? Maximum pages = 157
{12, 34, 67} | {90} ? Maximum pages = 113 (Optimal)

Test Case 2:
Possible Allocations:

{12} | {34} | {67, 90} ? Maximum pages = 157
{12, 34} | {67} | {90} ? Maximum pages = 90 (Optimal)
{12} | {34, 67} | {90} ? Maximum pages = 101
